frameworks.name: discrete attractor model
frameworks.one_line: memory states drift toward a small set of stable attractors

frameworks.name: diffusion (random-walk) noise
frameworks.one_line: delay-period activity also injects random diffusive error into memory

frameworks.name: load-dependent precision of working memory
frameworks.one_line: frontoparietal activity is known to track precision loss with load

core_question: How does delay-period activity in frontoparietal and occipital cortex relate to diffusion versus drift components of visual working-memory error?
key_assumptions: behavioral error decomposes into diffusion + attractor-driven drift
whole-trial fMRI activity can be linked to trial-wise error components
frontoparietal delay activity reflects memory maintenance
manipulations: delayed recall of line orientation across trials
key_findings: frontal and parietal delay activity increases with both diffusion and drift
diffusion explains more variance than drift in frontoparietal delay activity
in lateral occipital cortex, drift rather than diffusion dominates delay-period variance
interpretations: frontoparietal cortex preferentially indexes diffusive noise during maintenance
occipital cortex preferentially indexes systematic attractor drift
